title: Gagea bracteata
intro: Gagea bracteata, also known as the spring beauty, is a perennial plant belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae). The spring beauty has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gagea bracteata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gagea bracteata include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Gagea bracteata is a species of flowering plant in the family Liliaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant is classified as a bulbous perennial, characterized by its small white flowers and narrow leaves. Within the genus Gagea, Gagea bracteata is classified as part of section Gagea, which contains several species of similar plants. The classification of Gagea bracteata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Liliaceae family.
